description_long: This is a tall, narrow-necked ceramic vase with a bulbous body and a single curved handle connecting the neck to the shoulder. The surface is decorated with faded geometric and floral motifs, including a prominent four-petal flower and pairs of leaf-like shapes, all rendered in dark brown against a beige, weathered background. The neck is elongated and slightly flared at the mouth, with two dark horizontal bands encircling it. The handle is robust and smoothly integrated into the body, suggesting both decorative and functional intent. The vase shows signs of age, with discoloration and surface wear, giving it an antique, rustic appearance. Its overall form and ornamentation evoke traditional Mediterranean or Middle Eastern pottery styles.
description: A tall, antique ceramic vase with a bulbous body, narrow neck, and a single curved handle, adorned with faded floral and geometric motifs in brown on a beige surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the prominent four-petal floral motif and the full curvature of the bulbous body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the single curved handle and two leaf-like motifs, with the neck's angle more pronounced.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the flared mouth, handle attachment, and the arrangement of decorative bands around the neck.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the handle and the pair of leaf motifs, with less visibility of the main floral design.
